name: West Palm Beach Housing Authority
description_short: West Palm Beach Housing Authority provides project-based rental voucher assistance and housing programs for low-income and senior households in Palm Beach County, Florida.
description_long:
The West Palm Beach Housing Authority (WPBHA) is a government housing agency serving Palm Beach County, Florida. Established to address affordable housing needs, the organization administers federal housing programs and manages multiple residential communities. The WPBHA operates under HUD (Department of Housing and Urban Development) oversight and maintains compliance with federal income limits and program requirements.

WPBHA offers project-based voucher assistance programs tied to specific properties rather than portable across locations. Current programs include housing at Boynton Bay Apartments (2 and 3-bedroom units for heads of household 62+), Heron Estates (1-bedroom senior community for ages 62+), and other developments. The organization provides rental assistance to families whose household income falls within HUD-approved very low-income limits, typically at 50% of area median income. Applications are processed online only, with eligibility based on age requirements, income verification, and family composition. The WPBHA also offers resident services including Family Self-Sufficiency (FSS) programs, maintenance support, disaster preparedness resources, and domestic violence protections under VAWA.

WPBHA distinguishes itself as a non-profit public housing authority rather than a private landlord or for-profit service provider. The organization prioritizes vulnerable populations including seniors, persons with disabilities, and low-income families. Services are HUD-regulated and income-restricted, ensuring affordability standards. The authority maintains accessibility accommodations for persons with disabilities, including TTY services and application assistance.

The WPBHA is best suited for low-income residents of Palm Beach County seeking stable, affordable housing with income-based rental assistance. Applicants should note that availability is limited by unit openings, applications are processed on a first-come, first-served basis, and vouchers are property-specific rather than portable. This is a government program with HUD income and eligibility requirements, not a private rental service.
pros: HUD-approved program with strict income limits ensuring genuine affordability (50% area median income caps)
Project-based vouchers significantly reduce rental burden for eligible low-income households
Senior-focused communities available with age-62+ requirements for Boynton Bay and Heron Estates
Online-only application system allows 24/7 access without office visits or paper processing
Accessibility accommodations including TTY line (561-366-4900) and disability-specific support services
Family Self-Sufficiency (FSS) program helps residents build economic self-sufficiency beyond rental assistance
Transparent income limits published publicly; FY 2025 limits for Palm Beach County clearly disclosed
cons: Vouchers are property-specific and non-transferrable—assistance does not follow residents to other housing
Highly limited availability with long waiting lists based on first-come, first-served application timing
Strict income eligibility requirements may exclude households above HUD limits regardless of need
Online-only applications create barriers for applicants without internet access or digital literacy
Application processing timeline and unit availability uncertain; no guaranteed placement or timeframe provided
best_for: Low-income seniors aged 62+ in Palm Beach County seeking affordable rental housing
Families with household income at or below 50% of area median income ($40,950 for single person in 2025)
Persons with disabilities requiring affordable housing with accessibility accommodations
Residents of Riviera Beach or Boynton Beach with preference status for specific WPBHA properties
diagnosis: West Palm Beach Housing Authority is best for low-income and senior residents of Palm Beach County who qualify under strict HUD income limits and need affordable rental housing. The primary caveat is that availability is severely limited, application timing is critical (waitlists open on specific dates), and vouchers are tied to specific properties rather than portable—applicants must be interested in living at one of WPBHA's designated communities.
services: Project-based voucher assistance for Boynton Bay Apartments (2 and 3-bedroom units)
Project-based voucher assistance for Heron Estates (1-bedroom senior community)
Online application processing for housing waitlists
HUD income limit verification and eligibility determination
Family Self-Sufficiency (FSS) programs for resident economic development
Resident maintenance services and maintenance tip resources
Disaster preparedness and hurricane preparation resources for residents
Domestic Violence protections under VAWA (Violence Against Women Act)
TTY and relay services for persons with hearing impairments (561-366-4900)
Resident scholarships and community engagement programs
typical_results_timeline: Counseling available within 1-2 weeks of contact.
address: 3700 Georgia Ave, West Palm Beach, FL 33405
meta_description: West Palm Beach Housing Authority provides rental assistance and housing programs for low-income and senior households in Palm Beach County.
